entry.title: System not allowing to change Supplier number/code after creation of PO
entry.content:
How to change vendor or Supplier Number/code after creation of PO?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: System not allowing to change Supplier number/code af...
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, once a Purchase Order (PO) is created and saved, it is not possible to change the supplier (vendor) on that PO. The supplier field is a key field at the header level, and SAP does not allow changes to such key data after the PO has been saved.

This restriction exists because changing the supplier would affect multiple downstream processes such as approval workflows, output communication (PDF/email to supplier), goods receipt, invoice posting, and accounting. To maintain data integrity, the field is locked after creation.

What can you do instead?

  • If the PO has no follow-on documents (like GR or invoice), delete or cancel the PO and create a new one with the correct supplier.

  • If follow-on documents exist, reverse them (if allowed), cancel or close the PO, and then create a new one.

There is currently no SSCUI, BAdI, or API method available in Public Cloud that supports changing the supplier after PO creation.

If this is a critical business requirement, consider submitting an improvement request via the SAP Customer Influence Portal (SAP Note 2838428 provides the steps).

entry.title: Re: Global Running Day with SAP Community in Melbourne - Wednesday 4th June 2025
entry.content: Well done @pjcools and @Sandra_Solis - thank you for sharing the lovely photos!!

entry.title: Re: Global Running Day with SAP Community
entry.content:
Thanks for sharing @RajuRao !! And thank you to @pjcools. 
You can see more photos under this event post.

entry.title: Re: Title: SAP Business One – Need Dynamic Account Determ...
entry.content:
I don't think this is a good idea to implement account determination this way. For starters, when you are selling an item, you cannot distinguish an item based on how you got it, meaning that when selling you should know wheter you are selling purchased or produced unit, and post it accordingly to finished goods or raw material. Otherwise you will have an inconsistency in your trial balance and annual financial reports (in a situation where you purchase something as raw material, and then sell it as finished good).

You could theoretically set advanced account determination based on some UDF and then choose how you want each document to be posted, but you will still have the same problem as mentioned above. Some points for best practice in this matter:

  • Purchased item and produced item are never the same. You should always diferentiate them, preferably with separate item master data. If you absolutely must have them under single item code, atleast separate warehouses for raw materials and finished goods. And also block users from simply transferring item from one whse to another.
  • GL accounts for inventory posting should always be set by some application wide criteria. You cannot have inventory posting dependable on individual transactions and some possible values that can be changed by users on each transaction. You should set inventory account determination by either some item master attribute (item code, item group, some other UDF attribute of item master data) or some warehouse attribute (whse code or UDF on whse master data).

entry.title: 🚀 秒速で学ぶ SAP AI Core Agent 開発 🚀 - Part 8 CloudFoundry にデプロイ
entry.content:
生成 AI は研究室の外へ飛び出し、今やビジネス現場の常識を塗り替えています。SAPは全速力でその波に乗っています。このブログシリーズでは、SAP AI Core の既定モデルを最速で呼び出し、実務で使える AI エージェントへ拡張する“秒速ハンズオン”をお届けします。

お知らせ
You can find the English version here.

 

📖 本シリーズで学べること

  • SAP AI Core 上でカスタム AI Agent を “秒速” で動かす方法
  • LangChain・Google 検索ツール・RAGを使った実装
  • AI Agent を REST API 化し、SAPUI5/Fiori の UI に載せ、Cloud Foundryにデプロイする手順

学習時間
各章は 10–15 分 で読める＆手を動かせるを予定しています。

 

🗺️ 連載ロードマップ

  • Part 0 プロローグ
  • Part 1 環境構築: SAP AI CoreとAI Launchpad
  • Part 2 LangChain でチャットモデルを構築
  • Part 3 Agentツール: Google 検索を統合
  • Part 4 RAG 基礎 ①: HANA Cloud VectorEngineと埋め込み処理
  • Part 5 RAG 基礎 ②: Retriever Toolの構築
  • Part 6 AI Agent を REST API 化
  • Part 7 SAPUI5 でチャットUI を構築
  • Part 8 CloudFoundry にデプロイ [現在のブログ]

注記
続編は順次公開予定です。

この記事がお役に立ったら、ぜひ Kudos を押していただけると励みになります。 「ここをもっと詳しく知りたい」「別のテーマも取り上げてほしい」など、ご要望があればお気軽にコメントください！

CloudFoundry にデプロイ

1 | はじめに 

これまでローカルで開発してきた Python API と Fiori UI を、ビジネス向け PaaS である Cloud Foundry 環境へデプロイしましょう。

Cloud Foundry はアプリケーションのビルドパックを自動判定し、必要なランタイムを用意したうえでスケールやサービス連携を簡素化してくれるのが魅力です。今回は 「UI を mta build → cf deploy」「Python を manifest.yaml → cf push」 の 2 本立てで、“秒速” 本番デプロイを目指します。

 

2 | 事前準備

  • BTP サブアカウント
  • SAP AI Core インスタンス
  • SAP AI Launchpadのサブスクリプション
  • Python 3.13環境 & pip
  • VSCodeやBASなどのIDE

Trial 環境の注意
Trial の HANA Cloud インスタンスは 毎晩自動停止 されます。日をまたぐ作業の場合は翌日インスタンスを再起動してください

 

3 | Cloud Foundry にデプロイ

ローカルで完成した UI と API をクラウドへ持ち上げるために、まずはプロジェクトのフォルダ構成を再確認します。UI 側は MTA プロジェクトとして my-ai-agent-ui/ に、バックエンドの Python API は my-ai-agent-api/ に分かれているはずです。

# フォルダ構成
PROJECTS
├── my-ai-agent-ui/
│   ├── mta.yaml
│   └── ...
└── my-ai-agent-api/
    ├── main.py
    └── ...

 

最初にバックエンドからデプロイします。my-ai-agent-api 直下に manifest.yaml を用意し、必要なメモリやビルドパック、起動コマンドを宣言しておきます。

applications:
- name: my-ai-agent-api
  memory: 256M
  disk_quota: 2G
  instances: 1
  buildpack: python_buildpack
  command: gunicorn -w 1 -k uvicorn.workers.UvicornWorker main:app --bind 0.0.0.0:${PORT:-8000}
  random-route: true

 

次に Cloud Foundry CLI でサブアカウントへログインします。

cf login -a <CF_API_Endpoint>

次に、my-ai-agent-api フォルダで cf push を実行します。

cd my-ai-agent-api
cf push

デプロイが完了するとコンソールに routes: が表示され、そこが公開 URL になります。ブラウザで <route>/docs にアクセスして FastAPI の Swagger UI が開けば、API は正しくホストされています！

 

フロントエンドをデプロイする前に、チャット画面がクラウド上の API に正しく接続できるようエンドポイントを差し替えておきます。my-ai-agent-ui/webapp/controller/ChatEntity.controller.js の冒頭付近にある ENDPOINT 定数を、先ほど Cloud Foundry にデプロイした Python API の URL に置き換えてください。

  // 環境によってエンドポイントを切り替え
  const ENDPOINT = (
    ["localhost", "applicationstudio"].some(h => window.location.hostname.includes(h)) ||
    window.location.port === "8080"
  ) ? "" : "https://my-ai-agent-api-relaxed-raven-ie.cfapps.us10-001.hana.ondemand.com";

ローカル開発中は空文字列（UI5 ミドルウェアのプロキシ経由）を使い、Cloud Foundry にデプロイされたアプリが実行される環境ではフル URL が使われる仕組みです。

 

 続いて、フロントエンドを Cloud Foundry に載せます。my-ai-agent-ui フォルダに移動して mbt build を実行すると、mta_archives ディレクトリに MTAR ファイルが生成されます。生成物を cf deploy でアップロードすれば、UI 側もクラウドへ反映されます.

cd ../my-ai-agent-ui
mbt build
cf deploy mta_archives/my-ai-agent-ui_0.0.1.mtar -f

デプロイ後、BTP Cockpit の HTML5 Applications を開くと、先ほどのアプリがタイルとして表示されるのでクリックして起動します。 

もしチャットが API に接続できていなければ、ENDPOINT 定数や Destination の URL を先ほど控えた API ルートに合わせて修正してください。 

 

4 | チャレンジ – WorkZone に載せよう

SAP Build Work Zone, Standard Edition を使うと、今デプロイした HTML5 アプリを社内ポータルにワンクリックで追加できます。Integrate Your Application with SAP Build Work Zone, Standard Edition を参考に、次の流れでチャレンジしてみましょう。

  1. コンテンツ同期 — BTP Cockpit の Channel Manager で Fetch Updated Content を実行。
  2. Content Explorer でアプリを追加 — 対象アプリを選択し Add を押すだけ。
  3. グループ & ロール設定 — 例えば Everyone に割り当てて全社員へ公開。
  4. Site 作成 — Site Directory で新規サイトを作り、タイルをドラッグ&ドロップで配置。

 

5 | 終わりに

SAP BTP でカスタム AI を組む意義はシンプルです。業務データとモデルを同じ基盤に置けるため データ移送やガバナンス調整が最小限 になり、Fiori や Work Zone にそのまま埋め込めるので ユーザは慣れた SAP 画面から AI を呼び出せます。さらに MTA や AI Core が開発〜再学習までを一貫して支えるため、PoC から本番までの距離が短い——これが SAP でやる最大のメリットです。

今回の流れをテンプレート化しておけば、プロンプトと UI を差し替えるだけで新しい AI アプリを量産できます。ぜひ自社のユースケースに合わせて拡張してみてください！

ここまで、お疲れ様でした！ありがとうございました！

 

免責事項

本ブログに記載された見解および意見はすべて私個人のものであり、私の個人的な立場で発信しています。SAP は本ブログの内容について一切の責任を負いません。

entry.title: 🚀 SAP AI Core Agent QuickLaunch Series 🚀 - Part 8: Deploying to Cloud Foundry
entry.content:
Generative AI has leapt from research papers to daily business reality— and SAP is surfing that wave at full speed. In this hands‑on series, I’ll show you how to spin up a custom AI agent on SAP AI Core in minutes, then grow it into a production‑ready asset—without drowning in theory.

Notice
日本語版はこちらです。

 

📖 What You’ll Learn in This Series

  • How to Run a Custom AI Agent on SAP AI Core in Seconds
  • Implementation Using LangChain, Google Search Tool, and RAG
  • Steps to Convert the AI Agent into a REST API, Integrate It into an SAPUI5/Fiori UI, and Deploy to Cloud Foundry

Time Commitment
Each part is designed to be completed in  10–15 minutes .

 

🗺 ️ Series Roadmap

  • Part 0 Prologue
  • Part 1 Env Setup: SAP AICore & AI Launchpad
  • Part 2 Building a Chat Model with LangChain
  • Part 3 Agent Tools: Integrating Google Search
  • Part4 RAG Basics① HANA Cloud VectorEngine & Embedding
  • Part 5 RAG Basics ②: Building Retriever Tool
  • Part 6: Converting the AI Agent into a REST API
  • Part 7: Building the Chat UI with SAPUI5
  • Part 8: Deploying to Cloud Foundry [current blog]

Note
Subsequent blogs will be published soon.

If you enjoyed this post, please give it a  kudos! Your support really motivates me. Also, if there’s anything you’d like to know more about, feel free to leave a comment!

Deploying to Cloud Foundry

1 | Introduction

Let’s deploy the Python API and Fiori UI, which we've been developing locally, to a business-oriented PaaS environment: Cloud Foundry.

One of the key benefits of Cloud Foundry is its ability to automatically detect the appropriate buildpack, prepare the necessary runtime, and simplify tasks like scaling and service integration. In this session, we'll aim for a “lightning-fast” production deployment using a two-pronged approach:

  • For the UI: mta build → cf deploy
  • For the Python API: manifest.yaml → cf push

 

2 | Prerequisites

  • BTP sub-account
  • SAP AI Core instance
  • SAP AI LaunchPad Subscription
  • Python 3.13 and pip
  • VSCode, BAS or any IDE

Note for the Trial Environment
The HANA Cloud instance in the Trial enviroment automatically shuts down every night. If your work spans past midnight, please restart the instance the following day.

 

3 | Deploy to Cloud Foundry

To move the locally developed UI and API to the cloud, let’s first review the project folder structure. The UI should be set up as an MTA (Multi-Target Application) project under my-ai-agent-ui/, while the backend Python API is located in my-ai-agent-api/.

# Folder Structure:
PROJECTS
├── my-ai-agent-ui/
│   ├── mta.yaml
│   └── ...
└── my-ai-agent-api/
    ├── main.py
    └── ...

 

We’ll start by deploying the backend. Inside the my-ai-agent-api directory, create a manifest.yaml file to define the necessary settings such as memory allocation, buildpack, and startup command.

applications:
- name: my-ai-agent-api
  memory: 256M
  disk_quota: 2G
  instances: 1
  buildpack: python_buildpack
  command: gunicorn -w 1 -k uvicorn.workers.UvicornWorker main:app --bind 0.0.0.0:${PORT:-8000}
  random-route: true

 

Next, log in to your Cloud Foundry subaccount using the CLI:

cf login -a <CF_API_Endpoint>

Then, navigate to the my-ai-agent-api folder and run:

cd my-ai-agent-api
cf push

Once deployment is complete, the console will display a routes: section—this is your public URL. Open <route>/docs in your browser, and if the FastAPI Swagger UI appears, your API is successfully hosted!

 

Before deploying the frontend, make sure the chat screen can correctly connect to the API hosted on the cloud. Update the ENDPOINT constant near the top of my-ai-agent-ui/webapp/controller/ChatEntity.controller.js with the URL of the Python API you just deployed to Cloud Foundry. Replace it like this:

// Switch endpoint based on environment
const ENDPOINT = (
  ["localhost", "applicationstudio"].some(h => window.location.hostname.includes(h)) ||
  window.location.port === "8080"
) ? "" : "https://my-ai-agent-api-relaxed-raven-ie.cfapps.us10-001.hana.ondemand.com";

This setup uses an empty string during local development (so it routes through the UI5 middleware proxy), and switches to the full Cloud Foundry API URL when running in the deployed environment.

 

Next, let's deploy the frontend to Cloud Foundry. Move to the my-ai-agent-ui folder and run mbt build. This will generate an MTAR file in the mta_archives directory. Upload the build artifact using cf deploy to deploy the UI to the cloud:

cd ../my-ai-agent-ui
mbt build
cf deploy mta_archives/my-ai-agent-ui_0.0.1.mtar -f

After deployment, open HTML5 Applications in the BTP Cockpit. You’ll see the application displayed as a tile—click it to launch the UI.

If the chat interface fails to connect to the API, double-check the ENDPOINT constant in your code or the configured Destination URL. Make sure they match the API route you noted earlier.

 

4 | Challenge – Deploy to Work Zone

With SAP Build Work Zone, Standard Edition, you can easily add the HTML5 app you just deployed to an internal company portal with just a few clicks. Using the guide Integrate Your Application with SAP Build Work Zone, Standard Edition, try the following steps:

  1. Content Sync
    In the BTP Cockpit, go to Channel Manager and click Fetch Updated Content.
  2. Add App via Content Explorer
    Open Content Explorer, find your app, and click Add to include it.
  3. Set Groups & Roles
    Assign the app to a group—e.g., assign it to Everyone to make it available to all employees.
  4. Create a Site
    In Site Directory, create a new site and drag & drop the app tile into place on the layout.

 

5 | Conclusion

The value of building custom AI on SAP BTP is clear: by placing both your business data and AI models on the same platform, you minimize data transfer and governance overhead. Since you can embed the AI directly into familiar SAP interfaces like Fiori and Work Zone, users can interact with AI from the screens they already know.

Moreover, with tools like MTA and AI Core supporting the entire lifecycle—from development to retraining—you can move quickly from proof of concept to production. That’s the true strength of doing AI on SAP.

By turning this setup into a reusable template, you can rapidly create new AI applications—just swap out the prompt and UI to match different use cases. Feel free to expand on it to meet your company’s specific needs.

 

Thanks for following along, and great job making it this far!

 

Disclaimer

All the views and opinions in the blog are my own and is made in my personal capacity and that SAP shall not be responsible or liable for any of the contents published in this blog.

entry.title: Re: PTARQ Display Leave Requests with 0 absence hour
entry.content: any solution to it ?

entry.title: Re: SAP CAP, HDI and Datasphere
entry.content:
HI @manuelbordallo ,

You can expose datasphere objects as an API.
These APIS can be consumed in your CAP Application; of course, you need to create a destination for this in your BTP account.

entry.title: Rise With SAP - The FUE Game
entry.content:
Hello Friends & Colleagues. As you all are aware that now it is very tough time for all CIO & IT Leaders who had been using SAP as ERP Application which was back bone for their business. Due to shift from On Premise ECC / S4 Hana version we are now suppose to move towards Rise with SAP on Cloud or Private Cloud as an option.

It is very difficult situation for any leader on this point there is very limited choice which is now available with the customer.

Those who are currently working on SAP ECC Version have no choice to be on premise. Even if they convert on premise the conversion fees are very high and any new license procurement is very very high if compared with the previous license procurement value.

Those who had proactively converted their ECC Environment to S4 Hana earlier are also facing similar challenges due to contract interpretation where the contract is only for Hana Runtime and not for S4 Hana license conversion. As such there is conversion fees also applicable to S4 Hana customer which again is very high.

As you all will be aware that in case of Rise With SAP we have new type of license count which is called as FUE Charges which is total count of HB Professional (Ratio 1 to 1), Functional (Ratio 1 to 5) & Productivity (Ratio 1 to 30).

This calculation is on the basis of authorization object specified in SAP Note 3113382. 

The point which I want to highlight here is that SAP keep on changing the note version by incorporating / changing of Authorization Object / Activity in terms of reclassification from Lower to Higher Category of licenses.

As per the note version 89 a user who had authorization for posting MIRO Document was classified as HB Functional which was released in Feb'25. Now on 5th June'25 SAP has change the definition wherein the user who is having rights for MIRO (Authorization Object / Activity to create) is classified as HB Professional.

This is worry some as if such changes are been done to some one benefit the relevant customer budget would go for toss as they would have gone for Rise with certain calculation which is suddenly keep on changing and that change is northward - higher cost to pay.

The trust factor which we carried about this product is gradually going down due to such practices.

Just wanted to share the thought of what we are going through as I had been associated with software for last 25+ years.

Regards

Ketan

entry.title: Re: ABSENCE CARRY OVER
entry.content: Hi Clumbert, thank you so much for responding here. I have made corrections as suggested and could see the result as expected. However, the processed data doesn't seem to be coming to TT-ZQEC nor ABWKONTI data is updated with 104 hrs (displays 0). Any further suggestions please?

Does your taxonomy concept have enumerations? Maybe you can open a Support ticket, so you can share some files with me

 

Thanks

Marc

entry.title: Fiori Elements + RAP: How to preserve order of batch action calls? Wrong Batch order.
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm working on a Fiori Elements app based on RAP (ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model).
The user scans equipment one by one and then triggers a Print Label action.
The labels should be printed in the exact order the equipment was scanned, but sometimes they are printed in the wrong order.

What I observed:

  • The action printLabel is defined in the behavior definition.

  • When multiple rows are selected and the action is triggered, the backend method is called multiple times — once for each entity.

  • Although the UI sends a $batch, the printLabel method is executed separately per entity, and not necessarily in order.

This results in labels being printed in the wrong sequence, which causes confusion and extra manual effort.

My Behavior definition:

managed implementation in class ZBP_I_Equipment
strict(2);

define behavior for Z_I_Equipment alias Equipment
persistent table I_Equipment
lock master
{
    field( readonly ) Equipment;
    action printLabel;
}

 

My questions:

  1. Is there a way to ensure that printLabel is called once with all selected entries, preserving their original selection order?

  2. Is it possible to change the behavior to support multiple processing (e.g. on entity ... multiple) in this RAP setup?

  3. Are there best practices for preserving processing order in such scenarios?

Any guidance or working examples would be greatly appreciated.
Thank you!

entry.title: Re: ABAP Cloud Developer Trial 2023 Available Now
entry.content:
@GauravGaurav Normally only a new hardware key is "created" when a new instance/container is started out of an image. 
For an existing container the key should be stable. Not sure, but as far as I know the key depends on the Mac address and/or IP. That should be stable for an existing container. I would start investigating there. How do you restart/start your container?

entry.title: Re: Practice System for Recruiting Posting - THR83 - SAP...
entry.content:
Hi!

Recruiting Posting can only be enabled for VLC (Virtual Lead Class). I apologize for misleading information. However, with the upcoming materials updates ( in relation to 1H 2025 release) for each task, there will be a video showing you the steps. Hope this helps.

entry.title: Fiori APP / Odata V2 to API
entry.content:
I have created a FIORI App with classical odata V2.

I'd like to use API data with Odata by using CPI

Instead of 
APP (odata) -> destination back-end 
APP (OData) -> Destination CPI -> Use API

I suppose this is possible but I don't know exactly how to do that.

I have 2 sub-account. One for the APP, One for Integration Suite. 

Do you know steps to acheive that ? Ideally with an example.

Thanks gurus for your pov on this little challenge

entry.title: Re: Understanding ABAP Pipeline Engine used for Data Integration in Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi @Cocquerel , thanks for the question, will try to explain.

Master and Subscriber Logging tables has same fields but Key are different.

Master Logging table: has key as /1dh/sequence

Triggers ingest the data into the Master logging table post any changes in the source. The observer job /1dh/logtab is triggered, which does

1. ingest the data into Subscriber logging table

2. view reconstruction is executed, which selects the latest after images from the CDS view if there exists a corresponding key in at least one subscriber logging table

Subscriber Logging table:

has the key same as the key of base table. once it get filled, /1dh/push_delta_cds is triggered, which

1. Ingests the records based on the data (key of the changed data) that is present in Subscriber logging to buffer table or multiple buffer tables
2. It also makes sure that data between source and target are in sync by sending only delta records.

Since in the design of ABAP Pipeline engine, the observer and push delta jobs have different duties to be performed, and the actions are sequenced in the execution of these jobs.

Let me know your thoughts

entry.title: Re: ABAP Cloud Developer Trial 2023 Available Now
entry.content:
@TomHoepping  Below is the command I used to run in Ubuntu 25.04

sudo docker run --stop-timeout 3600 -it --name a4h -h vhcala4hci -p 3200:3200 -p 3300:3300 -p 8443:8443 -p 30213:30213 -p 50000:50000 -p 50001:50001 sapse/abap-cloud-developer-trial:2023 -skip-limits-check

After this I stop the container with ctrl+C, and start again with below command

sudo docker start -ai a4h

 It was working 4 months back, maybe some docker update has started this. I don't know.

I am looking forward to many more events. Cheers -Pawan

entry.title: C_BW4H_211 - SAP Certified Application Associate - Reporting, Modeling and Data Acquisition with SAP
entry.content: Which one of C_BW4H_211 and C_BW4H_214 is the most current and available certification to be taken in 2025? What are the differences, and what is the best road map to acquire/ the certification?

entry.title: Consuming a Third-Party SOAP Web Service in SAP Public Cloud via RAP
entry.content:
Dear All,

I hope this message finds you well.

In an SAP Public Cloud system, I am trying to consume a third-party SOAP web service using the RAP (RESTful ABAP Programming Model) framework.

While creating the destination, we encountered an error when providing the service URL directly.

I would like to understand the correct approach to follow if I were to use a Communication Scenario instead.

Unfortunately, I couldn't find any relevant resources on this topic. If anyone has experience with a similar case, your support would be highly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your assistance.

Best regards.

entry.title: Re: Inside ABAP Conf 2025: Innovation, Insight, and the Power of Community
entry.content:
Thanks for the write-up, Ben! I was in Vienna but we watched some of the sessions streamed from Mannheim too. I'm catching up on the sessions from other locations that I missed.

Great summary of the keynote, it gave me some food for thought as well. And you're right, it wasn't that simple back in the days either. ABAP work is never dull, that's for sure. 🙂

I wish we could have more in person events like this in the US, but I time and expense needed for travel is, sadly, a huge obstacle on our side of the pond.

entry.title: Re: Links to SAP demo recordings and key public presentations for HCM Business AI features
entry.content: Thank You.

entry.title: SAP MaxAttention IWS 'Unlocking SAP Business AI: A Technology Deep Dive' (July 8, 2025)
entry.content:
Virtual

July 8, 2025

Agenda

Dear Valued Customer,

We are pleased to invite you to the SAP MaxAttention Innovation Workshop focusing on “Unlocking SAP Business AI: A Technology Deep Dive” taking place virtually on July 8th, 2025. Discover how SAP Business AI can deliver transformative value across business processes. Explore the latest technologies and innovations to support your business in realizing value with AI. Please find the current agenda here.

This workshop will dive deep from a technology perspective on leveraging SAP Business AI to drive innovation and achieve impactful business outcomes. Through engaging keynotes, customer success stories, and expert-led discussions, attendees will gain a comprehensive understanding of SAP’s AI strategy and its practical applications:

  • Explore real-world use cases and see how the fundamentals of business have not changed, understand the principles of what AI agents are good at (and what they’re not), and what solutions leaders can leverage in an AI-first and agent-first world.
  • Hear firsthand experiences from customers on their SAP Business AI journey from exploration, roadmap build, and realization to enhance efficiency and drive success.
  • Learn about the evolution of Joule, SAP’s next generation UI, from Joule in SAP and non-SAP, Joule for Developers, Joule for Consultants, to extend Joule with Joule Studio.
  • Explore SAP AI Foundation, the AI operating system for all SAP Business AI solutions to make AI development and operations easier and more productive.
  • Learn how to design and build Graph-based Retrieval-Augmented Generation (GraphRAG) apps using SAP HANA Cloud’s Vector Engine and semantic graph capabilities
  • Gain insights into SAP MaxAttention’s role in accelerating AI adoption, including best practices and key learnings from successful AI projects.

Target audiences: IT Directors, Technical Architects, Development Architects, Data Scientists, etc.

Your customers will walk away with a clear understanding of how SAP Business AI can transform their business operations, drive efficiency, and enhance decision-making. SAP MaxAttention will support your customers throughout their AI journey, from initial planning to deployment, bridging the gap from AI Experimentation to AI Value realization.

Our Workshops

These sessions and workshop series focus on a specific topic and the purpose is to enable our customers to understand the product portfolio and innovative technologies of SAP in order to drive adoption and consumption. As specifically now, it is more important than ever to help companies to be successful and to respond to changing markets by transforming into intelligent enterprises in the experience economy.

Important

Please note that the 'Attend' button which can be found within this blog post does not serve as a formal registration for the event.

To officially register, please reach out to me, paul.schropp@sap.com and I will ensure that your registration is properly processed.

We look forward to having you join us for our upcoming events and thank you for your understanding!

Your feedback

Feel free to share your feedback and thoughts in the comment section below.

entry.title: Re: Inside ABAP Conf 2025: Innovation, Insight, and the Power of Community
entry.content: Kudos for the organisers and every speaker. Was a very cool event in Mannheim!

entry.title: API for Settlement rule creation in WBS Element
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Is there a standard API to create the settlement rule in WBS element by mass upload. 

Best Regards,

Channa

entry.title: Re: Title: SAP Business One – Need Dynamic Account Determ...
entry.content:
Hi @zak11 ,

Forgive me if I've missed any crucial detail here, but at the top of what has been answered (and I agree 100%), how would you deal with the accounting difference produced by such determination?

Let's start from zero. The G/L is brand new, and so is your physical stock.

Say you buy Item A, and it debits your Raw Materials G/L. When selling it, you credit Finished Goods G/L. When will the first get wiped? Are you manually writing it off? What about your FG, wouldn't it go inverted/negative as there was no previous debit balance in it?

BR,
Felipe

entry.title: Re: Managing enumeration in SAP DM stack 2201
entry.content:
Andrea,

 

Using entry point /efrag.org/xbrl/esrs/2022/esrs-all.xsd

Concept: art8:EnvironmentalObjectivesPursuedCapEx

 

You can see an enumeration on this:

 

In the DM_variables sheet, this is added in another column

 

Marc

entry.title: Partner Directory Accelerator Tool for Pipeline Concept
entry.content:
⚠️ Disclaimer: The helper tool described in this blog post is not an official product by SAP, but is available as SAP Open Source project, intended to accelerate the interaction with the Partner Directory for the Pipeline Concept.

 

Purpose

The Partner Directory Accelerator Tool supports the Pipeline Concept, which is introduced in a SAP Community blog by @alex_bundschuh and further documented in SAP Help. Before using this helper tool, please make yourself familiar with the Pipeline Concept itself.

The Pipeline Concept relies on data from the Partner Directory which is needed during message processing. The Partner Directory can be accessed through a user interface (UI) in the SAP Integration Suite (or via the Partner Directory API). Here, you can upload existing XSLT mappings after creating them manually, yet the Partner Directory UI itself does not support you in creating these XSLTs.

That is why the purpose of the helper tool is to accelerate the interaction with the Partner Directory for the Pipeline Concept. To do so, a Pipeline Concept-specific UI is provided which accesses the Partner Directory data via its API, allowing you to easily create or update XSLT mappings and other required content, and to replicate these Partner Directory entries to another tenant.

 

Key Features

XSLT Generation for Receiver Determination and Interface Determination

For receiver determination and interface determination, the Pipeline Concept offers different options on XSLT mappings, e.g., either one combined or multiple single XSLT mappings can be used. For both options, the helper tool supports the creation and maintenance of new and existing XSLT mappings. For that, a tabular input format is used in which you can maintain XPath conditions (if required) and the resulting receiver system / receiver interface. When triggering the generation of an XSLT, a XSLT syntax check is performed and the resulting XSLT is shown, which can be directly sent to the API.

 

Replication of Partner Directory Entries to another Tenant

In the helper tool, you can configure multiple tenants by providing respective credentials (which are only stored locally). Once you have configured more than one tenant, you can replicate Partner Directory entries to another tenant. To do so, select the relevant entries as well as the target tenant, and the tool takes over for you to create (and update if desired) these entries in your selected target tenant. One use case could be to replicate Partner Directory entries from a development tenant to a productive tenant.

 

Use the Tool

The tool is publicly available as SAP Open Source Project. All information on supported features, setup, usage, and contribution is maintained in this repository: 
https://github.com/SAP-samples/integration-suite-partner-directory-accelerator-for-pipeline-concept

entry.title: Can we do value mapping through MDG Consolidation ?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are doing vendor initial load through MDG consolidation from ECC source system to MDG Hub on S/4 HANA target system. Here the vendor account groups are changing in the target system (example: from ACGRP1 to ACGRP2)

Can anyone tell me if we can do this value mapping for a vendor from ACGRP1(ECC) to ACGRP2(S/4) while doing dataload through Consolidation process in MDG? 

Thanks in advance!! T

entry.title: Re: Error F0 750 in F110
entry.content:
Yes, I can confirm this. The issue will only come up, if too many addressees are added and the last(!) one is truncated so that no "@" is left.

If the @ remains, the other advices are still send to the complete mail addresses - only this one will show up in SOST with a red flag.

You can calculate the length of the actual INTAD field content plus the new email (and 1 for the needed space in between).

If it is too long, you can skip the address and protocol this in the payment protocol for the users.

It is however frustrating that in 2025 we still have a restriction of 130 characters for the recipients even in EHP8.

entry.title: Custom condition type is not visible in PO - Landed Costs
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have set up custom condition type for Custom Duty, so it can be added to the material price ( customer is using moving average),  i have set up calculation schema for purchasing, I have used existing Account key ( as i cant set up new one), it seems that I have done what needed to be done but condition is still not visible in the PO line items, so potentially I am missing something but cant workout what exactly is missing. Can someone please help?

 

Thanks

Barbara

entry.title: SAP BTP IS - Access Policies to restrict monitoring or object details?
entry.content:
Hi all, 
I've been seeing the capabilities of Access Policies in Integration Suite and that's clear. I've done a PoC and worked as intended. But... as intended is not enough.

I want to isolate not just the artifacts at  design time or just the change of traceability of deployed iflows when someone is operating the iPaaS. I want to fully isolate / restrict the objects. So If i have a pattern in policies that grants access to objects with the pattern 'AAA' the users that hasn't this, should not be able to:
- see that objects at design time (or at least the name of the objects in the corresponding list, but without access to the detail).
- Not able to monitor them. Now you can see them at monitoring tiles, even see the log. 

As explained in Package-Level Access Policy and in Artifact-Level Access Policy looks like this is the intended flow.  So what I'm trying to do is out of scope atm.

So, my point is, if i have a TeamAAA that has to monitors JUST the integrations with 'AAA' pattern how can I achieve it?

Exists the option to avoid the access to the object details or to exclude absolutely an iflow from monitoring? 

I want (wish 🙏 ) to be able to isolate them directly from SAP BTP IS. I've checked the roadmap and didn't see anything.

entry.title: Re: API for Settlement rule creation in WBS Element
entry.content:
I don't think there is an API.

Simone offers a possible solution here: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/bapi-to-create-and-delete-settlement-rules-in-wbs-element/qaq-p/683325/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/bapi-to-create-and-delete-settlement-rules-in-wbs-element/qaq-p/683325

In the past I have used LSMW's but I am not a fan of those generally.

entry.title: Re: ABAP Cloud Developer Trial 2023 Available Now
entry.content: @GauravGaurav  Start/Stop looks good to me. Did you checked the assigned IP Adress/Mac Adress of the container?

entry.title: Assigning Managers to Workers with Different Company Codes in Work Agreement Field "Manager"
entry.content:
We recently had the following question: "How to assign Managers outside of your Company Code? As whenever I click on the Manager field, only the Managers from the same Company Code are visible."


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Assigning Managers to Workers with Different Company...
entry.content: Click on the "Show Filters" button on top of the pop-up window, and remove the Company Code from the filter. This is a default setting, however it's also allowed/possible to select Managers from different company code.

entry.content:

  • Admin Center -

Hi everyone, on this topic I have a doubt.

If I assign the local accounting principle to ledger 0L, and IFRS accounting principle to ledger 2L, will it be possible to activate scope item 5HG? Or does the leading ledger 0L have to be associated to the IFRS accounting principle?

HTML5.SocketReadTimeoutInSeconds
WebIDEEnabled
WebIDESystem

entry.title: Re: Discover the Fresh New Design of SAP Help Portal
entry.content: I miss the tabs! But I am happy about the dark theme (even though it's well hidden)

entry.title: Centralised resilient high-volume goods label printing architecture
entry.content:
Hi,

At present our current Business Suite SAP systems typically print at various geographical sites via each site's Domain Controller, where printer services are hosted locally to supply the printing needs of that site.

SAP systems are non-RISE and hosted in the hyperscaler cloud.

Looking to the future the domain controllers at each site will be removed which means the local printing at each site will be impacted (lost) .

With this being the case we need to look at options for centralised printing options which are robust, resilient, performant i.e. logisitics sites need high volume printing at all times. Azure print seems to only support front-end print and SAP print in BTP doesn't appear to fit requirements and would be very costly 

Does anybody have a similar scenario and how did they overcome it?

thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: RAP Business Events with Advanced Event Mesh [2]: Creating custom business events
entry.content:
Hi Timo

After adding: @Event.context.attribute: 'xsapbaseunit'

there is no custom filter available in /n/IWXBE/EVENT_FILTER , also not visible in payload /n/IWXBE/EVENT_MONITOR.

I am not using AEM but EM as target broker.

appreciate further help !

Best regards,

Mateusz

entry.title: Scripting Error in WebGUI for HTML
entry.content:
When creating a personas flavor in WebGUI for HTML, scripting always fails, giving me an error like so:

sap.personas.scripting : Syntax error in script code:
SyntaxError: Unexpected token '<', "<!DOCTYPE "... is not valid JSON
at JSON.parse (<anonymous>)

This does not occur when using Slipstream Engine. Do I need to use SE if I have a scripting requirement? If not, any idea why I'm hitting this error and how to resolve it? I have not found any notes online for this issue.

 

ECC 6.8, Personas 3.0 SP17

entry.title: Re: Sum up values of MDF object on parent object
entry.content: @Sri_bs If the field is intended for displaying the data on the UI, this would be okay. After changes are saved, the onLoad rules are executed again before the saved object is displayed on the UI.

entry.title: SAP FIORI - Upload General Journal Entries
entry.content:
Hello everyone, 

When using the Fiori App "Upload General Journal Entries" to upload general journal entries via Excel, I receive the following error message: 

UI5-Komponente for navigation view "#FinanceApplicationLog-showList?DateFrom=&DateTo=&LogExternalId=Header&LogObjectId=FIGL_UPLDGLJE&LogObjectSubId=&PersKey=fin.gl.journalentry.upload.ShowLog&sap-app-origin-hint=&sap-xapp-state=TAS21Q6XL0RC1NE8F29FAEWYBAI18Kxxxxxxxxx" could not be loaded. 

Did anyone face a similar situation or found a solution?

 

Best 

Martin

Adem

Chuma

entry.title: Re: Basic Authentication Fails with “Bad Credentials” Whe...
entry.content:
Hi.

I'd recommend using Google before asking questions to the community, this is a common error that has been answered a lot of times. You can have a look at Setting up inbound HTTP connections - Integration flow processing. You're basically trying to use a client ID and secret as a user and password, when you need to get a token to call the iflows. You can see that the www-authenticate header is telling you it expects a Bearer.

Also I'd recommend deleting that service key and instance and creating a new one. You're basically opening up your tenant to anyone by posting your service key here.

entry.title: Re: IS -Media on S4 HANA
entry.content: IS MEDIA solutions are not available in S/4HANA

entry.title: Re: Can I add an annotation to a standard SAP CDS view if it does not affect compatibility?

entry.title: Re: Migration for Asset Under Construction in S/4 HANA Pu...

entry.title: Re: Can I add an annotation to a standard SAP CDS view if...

entry.title: Re: Mass update field Credit Standing Institute(SOL_INS) in BP role UKM000
entry.content: Hey, did you find solution for this?

entry.title: Bulk Record Insertion into Database Using Groovy Script in CPI
entry.content:
Bulk Record Insertion into Database Using Groovy Script in CPI

  • Bulk records can be inserted into a database from SAP CPI using a Groovy script that converts XML input data into SQL insert queries for JDBC execution .
  • A sample scenario involves three employee records with unique IDs and names, passed as XML payload to the Groovy script for processing .

Groovy Script Functionality and Workflow

  • The Groovy script reads the input XML containing multiple employee records and iterates over each record using a loop to generate individual SQL insert statements 
     
  • The script constructs insert queries dynamically by appending SQL syntax and field values extracted from the XML, handling both string and numeric data types appropriately  .
  • String values are enclosed in single quotes in the SQL query, while numeric values are inserted without quotes to ensure correct data typing in the database .
  • This looping and appending process repeats for all records in the XML, creating a batch of insert queries for bulk insertion 
     

Integration with CPI and JDBC Adapter

  • The Groovy script outputs the complete SQL insert queries, which are then executed by the JDBC adapter configured with a database alias in CPI to insert data into the target database table .
  • The process does not require explicit mapping in CPI since the Groovy script handles the conversion from XML to SQL queries directly  .
  • The example flow uses a scheduler to trigger the job and a content modifier to provide the XML payload as input to the Groovy script  .

Verification and Results

  • Before insertion, the database is checked to confirm that the employee records with the specified IDs do not already exist, ensuring no duplicates.
  • After running the flow, the Groovy script successfully inserts all employee records in bulk into the database, verified by querying the database and confirming the presence of the new records 
     

Practical Implications and Usage

  • This approach allows efficient bulk insertion of records in SAP CPI, reducing the overhead of inserting records one by one and improving performance.
  • The Groovy script is reusable for different XML inputs and can be adapted for various bulk database insertion requirements in CPI integration projects 
     
ℹ️ Note: Using Groovy scripting to generate SQL insert queries enables dynamic handling of input data structures and types, facilitating flexible and scalable database operations within CPI integration flows.
 
SAP Cloud for Customer integration with SAP S/4HANA  Cloud Integration SAP Cloud Integration for data services SAP Cloud for Customer, server-side integration

entry.title: Re: SAP Activate for SAP Sustainability Solutions Portfol...
entry.content: Adding an answer here since comments seems to have resolved question.

entry.title: Re: SAP Activate for SAP Sustainability Solutions Portfol...
entry.content: Adding an answer here to be able to move the question to answered since comments appear to have resolved the question.

entry.title: Re: Treating Business Goals as "Individual" Goals possible?
entry.content:
@mkrieg I can't think of a way to automate the retrieval of these 2 field values (billable target and actual billable hours currently in Comp Info) into a PM form (directly or through Goal Management).

I would recommend permanently moving the maintenance of these fields to a dedicated Goal Plan in the future to make the design easier.

In the meantime here is the best design option I can think of:

1. Create an integration center job to send the Comp Info fields to an effective-dated MDF object.

2. Move the billable section into a "Business Section" of Variable Pay (instead of a team section)

3. Edit your varpayemphist background-element to add 3 new fields:

  • Billable Hours Target (type ffld): mapped to the right MDF field
  • Billable Hours Actual (type ffld) : mapped to the right MDF field
  • BusGoal Assignment (type ifld) : leave mapping blank

4. Create a Business Goal assigned through attribute = "BusGoal Assigmment" = 0 (so that everyone gets it)

5. Create 2 assignment level custom fields in the business goals section that get their values directly from the varpayemphist background-element : Billable Hours Target, Billable Hours Actual. 

6. Create 1 assignment level custom field in the business goals section that calculates the achievement based on the two fields from #5.

7. Roll the result from #6 to the summary level (either copy paste the ID from the BusGoal section achievement custom field into an entry level custom column if all you need is the result from the last record - assuming the billable achievement doesn't change throughout the year - or do more complex calculations to apply section weigths or divide by number of records).

8. Add it to the final payout at entry level.

 

 

There may be other options with an integration expert to help create a technical integration between EC comp info and PM but I'm not even sure it is possible, I've never heard of a solution where you take a field in EC and turn into the attribute of a Goal in a goal plan through integration.


Best,

Xavier

entry.title: Re: SAP Activate for SAP Sustainability Solutions Portfol...
entry.content: Hi, Paul, I marked this answered but in case you disagree, you can un-accept it anytime if you have further question. Thank you for bringing this question to SAP Community! Best regards, Paula

entry.title: Re: Custom condition type is not visible in PO - Landed C...
entry.content:
Hello @BarbaraFlis 

If your custom condition type for customs duty isn't showing in the PO line items, check these key points:

  • Go to SSCUI 101117 – Maintain Calculation Schemas and ensure your condition type is added to the relevant schema.
  • Make sure the condition is either manual (set in SSCUI 103029) or has a condition record if automated.
  • Verify that the schema is correctly assigned to your vendor’s schema group and document type.

For landed cost to update material price (MAP), the condition type must also have “Material Update” checked.

Helpful SAP References:

  • SAP Help: Condition Technique in Purchasing
  • SAP Note 2667340 – Custom condition not shown in PO
  • S/4HANA Public Cloud Maintain Calculation Schemas (101117)

Thank you and kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Error in outbound IDoc: "EDI: Field ARCKEY in control record with value"
entry.content:
Hi!

Our team is currently trying to establish connection between BTP and S4HANA via IDoc adapter. We already made the inbound connection work. but encountered an error during the outbound flow. We received the following error:

"EDI: Field ARCKEY in control record with value 'urn:sap.com:msgid=<MESSAGE ID>'". The message ID part is a 27-character alphanumeric code.

Is there a way to remove this field? Or a way for this field to automatically not populate any value? I tried manually removing the value via WE19 and it worked, so I figured removing this field is the only way. We cannot find a KB that could solve this issue. 

Thank you very much!

entry.title: Re: Mass update field Credit Standing Institute(SOL_INS) in BP role UKM000
entry.content: Hey - Just checking if you found any solution to this?

entry.title: Contract request fails to send to SAP S/4HANA via CIG with Error 500
entry.content:
We have created a contract workspace in Ariba CLM, and the CLID document has been published. We are now attempting to integrate the contract request into SAP S/4HANA using the standard add-on integration via CIG. All required configurations have been completed, including the setup of endpoints from the SAP Business Network. However, the error occurs immediately upon sending and does not even reach CIG or the Ariba Business Network.

When we click "Send to External System", we receive the following error:

"Contract Request document could not be sent to the external system. Error Message: Status code: 500, status text: Internal Server Error, status message: Integration Error."

This is the only entry recorded in the document's History tab.

From the Contract Workspace Overview, under External System Configuration, we have the following values:

  • Company Code: XXXX

  • Purchasing Organization: XXXX

  • Purchasing Group: XXXX

  • Document Type: Contract

  • Document Category: WK Value Contract

Could someone please confirm if the Document Type and Document Category values are correct? We're unsure if these are contributing to the error. Any assistance would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Error in establishing IDoc connection between SAP CPI...
entry.content: I did the first one and put a constant '1' to all segments. This is very helpful, Raju. Thank you very much!

entry.title: Re: Develop Powerful Analytical Queries using ABAP Core Data Services (CDS)
entry.content:
Hello All,

I have a question:

from what I know, you can not specify a cache in the BEx query generated by the activation of CDS analytical projection view, as they are based on a transient provider.

What are caching strategies available other than Dynamic Cache object?

Best Regards,

Alberto

entry.title: Re: SAP FIORI - Upload General Journal Entries
entry.content:
FinanceApplicationLog-showList

probably this app is not activated.

entry.title: Re: Consuming a Third-Party SOAP Web Service in SAP Public Cloud via RAP
entry.content: Hello @dogukanpadel Can you tell us what error / provide a screenshot of the error so we can check what could be causing it ?

entry.title: Re: ABSENCE CARRY OVER
entry.content: Glad that helped. I think you will need to add another CUMBT function after your ZQEC rule. That function will write the data from the TES table over to the ZES table that will be available for your ZQEU rule. The only issue that I have ever seen with adding a 2nd CUMBT is if the schema uses ADDMB within the day processing, which it should not.

entry.title: Filling the Gaps: Enhancing SAP’s ONB 2.0 to Learning Pre-Hire Integration Guide
entry.content:
Filling the Gaps: Enhancing SAP’s ONB 2.0 to Learning Pre-Hire Integration Guide

The community post for the integration of learning with onboarding to enable access to courses for Pre-Hires outlines the essential requirements for integrating Onboarding and SuccessFactors Learning. It is possible to build upon this foundation to integrate the learning experience for your Pre-Hire user population more deeply. This can be accomplished by adding additional configuration to segment the Pre-Hire population from employee learning assignments and define a user experience that both provides the experience appropriate for Pre-Hires, as well as protects proprietary company information that is intended for your employee population. This blog will focus on the configuration required to accomplish these goals.

Baseline Configuration: ONB 2.0 to LMS

1. Extract data from Employee Central using Integration Center

  • Create a simple output integration file to export the Onboarding New Hire user data
  • Select starting entity = ONB2Process

 2. Learning system configuration

  • Modify the “SF User Connector” properties file in the Learning system configuration

Note, the corrected settings from Step 2 Details are:

  • Search for this Configuration Key“sfuser.field.transform.data.NOTACTIVE.N” and add “active_external_suite”  with existing statuses
  • Search for this Configuration Key“sfuser.field.transform.data.NOTACTIVE.Y” and add “inactive_external_suite” with existing statuses

3. Schedule the Integration Center Job to export User data for Learning

4. Schedule the Learning SF USER CONNECTOR to import the Pre-Hire user information

The additional configuration to further define what a Pre-Hire can access and how their profiles are created and managed in Learning can be broken down into two functional components: Learning configuration and Integration Center configuration.

Configuration Requirements for Learning

Segmenting ONB Pre-Hires into a separate Security Domain excludes the Pre-Hire Onboarding population from any existing assignment profiles, as the Security Domain is not a subdomain configured in existing assignment profiles.  Creating an ONB User role provides control over what the Pre-Hire can access in Learning, different from that of an existing employee.

1. ONB Security Domain ID

  • Create a new root-level Security Domain that the Integration will put all ONB users into. For example, ONB_DMN

2. ONB Role ID

  • The Role ID that the Integration will assign to anyone in Learning as a Pre-Hire. This role ID should be limited to the bare level of access required. Typically, this means assigning permissions that allow them to access assigned training and learning history, but excluding permissions such as report access, library search, bookmarks, My team, and Easy Links.

3. Update Learning CONNECTORS

  • sfuser.connector.defaultValue.studentRoleID=NULL
  • sfuser.connector.input.file.multiple=true

4. Create an Assignment Profile for Pre-Hire learning assignments.

  • Add Security Domains: Security Domains = ONB_DMN
  • Setup Rules Domain ID = ONB_DMN

Configuration Requirements for Integration Center:

Adding in the default Security Domain and Learning User role to the ONB Integration, and the existing Learning User sync manages the users uniquely and separately depending on their intended access to Learning. Once the Pre-Hire completes their Onboarding process, they will be included in the standard Learning User sync population. When this occurs, their domain and role will be updated to the configured domain and role in the employee file. It is important to note that all the learning history from the Onboarding process remains with the employee's user record. Any assignments that were not completed would be removed when they no longer match the ONB Assignment Profile(s).

1. ONB Integration Job

  • Change the Effective Date on the Filter and Sort tab to 2099-12-31
  • Add DMN_ID and ROLE_ID to the Header row(s)
  • Set the Default Values as “ONB_DMN” and “ONB_USER" respectively.
  • Change the File Extension to txt.2 on the Destination Settings tab.

2. Existing Learning User Sync Job

  • Add DMN_ID and ROLE_ID to the Header row(s)
  • Set the Default Value to the employee Domain ID and employee Role ID.
  • In the destination settings, change the File Extension to txt.1

Note: If your organization uses a more complex Security Domain Hierarchy for employees, the existing mapping for the DMN_ID should be retained and not updated with a default value.

3. Schedule the Integration Center Job for the Onboarding Pre-Hire Integration. This should be scheduled to align with the existing Learning Employee Central user sync, as both files will be processed in Learning sequentially. 

4. Schedule the User Connector - SF job to pick up and process both files. The results are visible in “View Results” with each file process listed individually.

In addition to these required configuration updates, a leading practice is ONB Pre-Hire Users should not have a manager indicated on their User Profile. If a manager is added to the ONB User Profile, that manager will be able to manage (assign or remove) training to the Pre-Hire. Since Onboarding in SuccessFactors is a pre-hire status, the training assigned to the user should be carefully reviewed. It is possible that an Onboarding Pre-Hire user does not convert to an employee in the case of a failed background check, rescinded job offers, or other reasons. Your organization should carefully review the appropriate information for training to be assigned and shared before the conversion to an employee status.

 

For more Information, please see the SAP Help section for this integration

entry.title: Re: Standard Site URL in RMK
entry.content: Very informative.

entry.title: Re: Create a Regression Testing Package in Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Hi, Manuelli,
I see your question is remaining unanswered here, so I will attempt to reply with answer here.
Project is required....you cannot create test cases without a connection to a project in Cloud ALM.

When you want to use a test case with another project, you have the ability to 'download' the project:

 

and then you can navigate the new Project, you can upload this (browse to the downloaded file from above) and it will import and display in the new project's assigned test cases:

I hope this closes the loop for you.

Best regards,
Paula
